violations.description: Tests create mappings and receipt rows but do not perform explicit teardown, relying on uniqueness and retention windows for isolation.
violations.suggestion: Add optional test-scope cleanup hooks (or reset helpers) for long-running shared environments to keep state bounded.
violations.code_snippet: const mappingResponse = await apiRequest(request, { method: 'POST', path: storyE5Context.paths.numbersCollection, ... });

violations.description: Factory defaults use stable tenant/orgUnit identifiers; this is practical but can increase cross-run coupling in persistent shared test environments.
violations.suggestion: Allow easy override to per-run tenant/orgUnit in environments where data reset is not guaranteed.

recommendations: Introduce optional teardown/reset utility for mappings/receipts
Keep fixture-based context generation to preserve test independence
Preserve unique deterministic event/message IDs to avoid collisions
summary: Isolation is good with fixture-based context and unique IDs; explicit cleanup strategy could be strengthened for long-lived shared environments.
